title: Mildly Spiced Middle Eastern Lentil Stew
description: A hearty and aromatic stew featuring red lentils, mirepoix, and a blend of Middle Eastern spices, perfect for a crowd, this fermented and aerated dish is sure to please. It combines the warmth of chili powder with the comfort of a traditional stew.
instructions: In a large pot, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion, carrot, and celery and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 10 minutes.
Stir in the chili powder, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper and cook for 1 minute.
Add the lentils, vegetable broth, and 2 cups of water to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes.
Use an immersion blender to puree the stew to the desired consistency, or allow it to cool and puree it in a blender.
Return the stew to the pot and reheat it over low heat, if necessary.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
